content.claim: Cross-country econometric evidence shows early tracking at ages 10–12 increases measured educational inequality; the effect on mean national performance is contested, with mathematics results consistently lower under early tracking but statistical significance inconsistent across studies.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Education finance lens
limitations: Meta-analysis finding on mean performance effect reported at 10% significance level in one of three comparisons, indicating weak and inconsistent aggregate efficiency claims
International assessments may not fully capture all skill dimensions
Causal estimates assume selection on observables; residual confounding possible
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
